_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to define caching and analyze the information contained within
reference_document_element_description: Monitor data quality within the assigned domain, document issues, initiate remediation workflows, and track resolution to closure.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-T-016 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-S-006 (DA, cosine: 0.451), RC-T-006 (RC, cosine: 0.414).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.205). Shared vocabulary: [within]. DCWF-only terms [ability, analyze, caching, contained, define, information] vs WIDAI-only terms [assigned, closure, data, document, domain, initiate] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.882, R=0.851). P exceeds R by 0.031 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Ability to define caching and analyze the information contained within" and "Monitor data quality within the assigned domain, document issues, initiate remed" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.486 > STS 0.205 (gap +0.280). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [within] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.4%. Shared tokens: [within]. DCWF-only: [ability, analyze, caching, contained, define, information]. WIDAI-only: [assigned, closure, data, document, domain, initiate].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.280 with low STS 0.205. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
